title: (No AI) TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
author: Jay Peters
content:
![The TikTok logo on a black background with pink and blue repeating logos around the edges.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/23951408/STK051_VRG_Illo_N_Barclay_4_tiktok.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted [by social media consultant Matt Navarra](https://x.com/MattNavarra/status/2078129989128450064). The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells _The Verge_. YouTube has been working on [a similar tool](https://www.theverge.com/news/803818/youtube-ai-likeness-detection-deepfake) and recently [made it available to all adult users](https://www.theverge.com/news/931884/youtube-likeness-detection-ai-deepfake-expansion-all-adults).

Creators who are part of TikTok's test and want to use the tool will first have to verify their identity with [a company called Jumio](https://www.jumio.com/about/). You'll have to do a real-time selfie scan and an ID check, but Kizer says that "TikTok does not retain ID documents, and facial informati …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/967486/tiktok-ai-likeness-detection-tool)
contentSnippet:
TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted by social media consultant Matt Navarra. The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells The Verge. YouTube has been working on a similar tool and recently made it available to all adult users.
Creators who are part of TikTok's test and want to use the tool will first have to verify their identity with a company called Jumio. You'll have to do a real-time selfie scan and an ID check, but Kizer says that "TikTok does not retain ID documents, and facial informati …
Read the full story at The Verge.

The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells The Verge. YouTube has been working on a similar tool […]

title: (No AI) Apple’s plot to crush OpenAI
author: David Pierce
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/VRG_VST_071626_Site.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

[Apple is suing OpenAI](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964350/apple-openai-lawsuit-trade-secrets). The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just [the ways things are done](https://www.businessinsider.com/apple-lawsuit-openai-trade-secrets-what-smart-people-are-saying-2026-7). So what does Apple really want here, and why is it picking such a public fight with OpenAI?

On [this episode of _The Vergecast_](https://pod.link/vergecast), Nilay and David go through the lawsuit, and look at Apple's history of splashy litigation to determine whether Apple is worried about a possible competitor or simply looking to capitalize on [a weak moment for OpenAI](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/965294/openai-apple-trade-secrets-lawsuit-sam-altman-ipo). All this is happening as Apple [ships the public betas](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964307/apple-public-betas-ios-27-siri-ai) of its new software, headlined by [the new Siri AI](https://www.theverge.com/tech/964714/siri-ai-public-beta-preview-ios-27-hands-on), and we have though …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/podcast/967244/apple-openai-lawsuit-vergecast)

Apple is suing OpenAI. The complaint is readable and intense, as these things often are, though many experts seem to think many of the allegations are just the ways things are done. So what does Apple really want here, and why is it picking such a public fight with OpenAI?
On this episode of The Vergecast, Nilay and David go through the lawsuit, and look at Apple's history of splashy litigation to determine whether Apple is worried about a possible competitor or simply looking to capitalize on a weak moment for OpenAI. All this is happening as Apple ships the public betas of its new software, headlined by the new Siri AI, and we have though …
Read the full story at The Verge.

creator: Matt Burgess
title: (No AI) San Francisco Demands Apple and Google Delete AI ‘Nudify’ Apps From App Stores
dc:creator: Matt Burgess
content: The City Attorney’s Office sent the tech giants cease-and-desist letters this week telling them to stop profiting from 13 “face-swap” apps that are overwhelmingly used to target women and girls.
contentSnippet: The City Attorney’s Office sent the tech giants cease-and-desist letters this week telling them to stop profiting from 13 “face-swap” apps that are overwhelmingly used to target women and girls.
